verses.text: King Lemuel records his mother's teaching: 'give not thy strength unto women,' nor your ways to that which destroys kings. A mother warns her royal son. Even kings must guard against the ruin of unbridled lust.

King Lemuel records his mother's teaching: 'give not thy strength unto women,' nor your ways to that which destroys kings. A mother warns her royal son. Even kings must guard against the ruin of unbridled lust.

It is not for kings to drink wine, lest they forget the law and pervert the judgment of the afflicted; give strong drink rather to the perishing and grieved. Rulers must keep clear heads. Sobriety is needed where justice is in their hands.

'Open thy mouth for the dumb' and for all appointed to destruction; judge righteously and plead the cause of the poor and needy. Speak up for the voiceless. A ruler's duty is to defend those who cannot defend themselves.

'Open thy mouth for the dumb' and for all appointed to destruction; judge righteously and plead the cause of the poor and needy. Speak up for the voiceless. A ruler's duty is to defend those who cannot defend themselves.

'Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.' Her husband trusts her, lacking nothing, and she does him good all her days. A godly wife is a rare treasure. Her worth is beyond all jewels.

'Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.' Her husband trusts her, lacking nothing, and she does him good all her days. A godly wife is a rare treasure. Her worth is beyond all jewels.

'Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.' Her husband trusts her, lacking nothing, and she does him good all her days. A godly wife is a rare treasure. Her worth is beyond all jewels.

She works willingly with her hands, brings food from afar like merchant ships, and rises while it is yet night to provide for her household. She is diligent and provident. Her industry begins before dawn.

She works willingly with her hands, brings food from afar like merchant ships, and rises while it is yet night to provide for her household. She is diligent and provident. Her industry begins before dawn.

She works willingly with her hands, brings food from afar like merchant ships, and rises while it is yet night to provide for her household. She is diligent and provident. Her industry begins before dawn.

She considers a field and buys it, plants a vineyard with her earnings, girds herself with strength, and sees that her goods are good, her lamp not going out by night. She is wise and enterprising. Her labour is steady and her judgment sound.

She considers a field and buys it, plants a vineyard with her earnings, girds herself with strength, and sees that her goods are good, her lamp not going out by night. She is wise and enterprising. Her labour is steady and her judgment sound.

She considers a field and buys it, plants a vineyard with her earnings, girds herself with strength, and sees that her goods are good, her lamp not going out by night. She is wise and enterprising. Her labour is steady and her judgment sound.

She works the spindle, and 'she stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy'; she fears no snow for her household, for they are clothed in scarlet. She is generous and prepared. Her hands serve both her home and the poor.

She works the spindle, and 'she stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy'; she fears no snow for her household, for they are clothed in scarlet. She is generous and prepared. Her hands serve both her home and the poor.

She works the spindle, and 'she stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy'; she fears no snow for her household, for they are clothed in scarlet. She is generous and prepared. Her hands serve both her home and the poor.

She makes herself fine coverings and clothes of silk and purple; her husband is known in the gates among the elders; she makes and sells linen and girdles to the merchants. She adds beauty and enterprise. Her work brings honour to her home.

She makes herself fine coverings and clothes of silk and purple; her husband is known in the gates among the elders; she makes and sells linen and girdles to the merchants. She adds beauty and enterprise. Her work brings honour to her home.

'Strength and honour are her clothing,' and she shall rejoice in time to come; 'she openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness'; she watches over her household and eats not idle bread. Her character is her adornment. Wisdom and kindness mark her speech.

'Strength and honour are her clothing,' and she shall rejoice in time to come; 'she openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness'; she watches over her household and eats not idle bread. Her character is her adornment. Wisdom and kindness mark her speech.

'Strength and honour are her clothing,' and she shall rejoice in time to come; 'she openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness'; she watches over her household and eats not idle bread. Her character is her adornment. Wisdom and kindness mark her speech.

Her children rise up and call her blessed, and her husband praises her: many have done well, 'but thou excellest them all'; 'favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.' The fear of God is her true beauty. Let her works praise her in the gates.
